## [00:00 - 03:03]

This lesson is all about the promotion. And promotion is all about brand building. It doesn't matter if it's your personal brand, the company's brand, the team's brand, the brand for one of your particular services or a newly acquired business or the product, it doesn't matter. Look, promotion isn't about a thing you do. Promotion is everything you do. See, if you recognize that in all you do, you always have to be promoting, then you'll build a healthy, valuable business. Grant Cardone talks about better known always beats best. He also says those that don't know you won't flow you. So think about this. When you're promoting your business, what should you be promoting? The answer is easy. Everything. What you do, who you do it for, the benefit of people working with you, the benefit of people working for you, the benefit of the impact of what you do. Promoting employees up your hierarchical channel so that they can go and build and run the company for you. Promoting is promoting. Promote, promote, promote. Always be promoting. Even when you don't feel like promoting, promote more. You know why? The more you put out in the universe, who you are, what you do, why you do it, the benefit of doing it with you, the benefit of doing it for you, the benefit of doing it around you, the impact of what you're doing, the more you put that out in the universe, the more comes back. See, outflow amplifies inflow. So the more you put out, the more you get back. And as a 10x owner, you need to constantly be in the promotional frame of mind. This will do another thing for you. Say you come to work and something's really bugging you, right? Maybe you're mad. Maybe the bank just called and said, you're running late on that first payment and you just got served that first little deal from the bank that you violated your covenants and now you're pissed off, okay? Well what you need to do is you need to convert that energy to promotion. You need to find a way to take what's happening in the moment that's working against you and you need to convert it to promoting it for you. And the way you do that is promote to yourself. Hey, how could I be a crappy business owner that would put myself in a position where I would violate a covenant? So I'm going to go out and promote. You know why we're going to pay attention to our numbers. You know why we're going to pay attention to the key performance indicators. You know why I'm going to get all over my accountant? I'm going to promote the fact that I want to run a healthy, successful, profitable, trustworthy business. That's promotion. The art of promotion lies at the heart of all brand building and all business relationships.

## [03:03 - 06:04]

My friend and partner, Grant Cardone, knows this all too well. In fact, the majority of what I'm talking about when it comes to promotion, I have learned since partnering with Grant. See I wish I would have known what I know today and you know for me I was always a promoter but I didn't actually understand the depth of what it means to promote until I partnered with Grant Cardone and he taught me. So as successful as I was, if I'm conceding to you that I didn't understand the significance and importance and process of promoting, then you can concede you've got something to learn from this program. Now Grant can often be heard saying, if people don't know you, they can't flow you. I quoted that earlier but that was one of the first things I heard when I went to my first growth con and I was like, man here I am wanting to get massive. I know I have an unbelievable value proposition for business owners and yet I don't know how to get in front of them. And he said those words and I was like, that's my problem. People don't know me so they can't flow me. A 10x business owner embraces the lifestyle of massive perpetual promotion in all aspects of their life. So if you want to be an unbelievably successful business owner with a profitable business with happy people working with you, happy people working for you, then start promoting that now. Promote it to yourself, promote it to your team, promote it to anybody that'll listen. Go stand on the tallest building in your town and scream it as loud as you can. Scream it because you have to own it. See promotion is your willingness to own something so important and so significant that all you do is talk about it. Now here's what Grant says are the four reasons to promote. Promote for yourself. To remind yourself you're in business every single day. Number two, promote for your employees. Promote them that you're committed and serious about building a huge business. Look the owner that sold you the business probably didn't do any of this so they're going to feel like you are serious and they're going to be excited to be on a winning team. Third, promote for your competitors so they know you're there, especially as a new business owner. Hey I'm showing up man. I'm coming. I'm coming baby, step your game up. Put some pressure in the system by promoting to your competitors. And then fourth, promote for new customers so they can connect with your brand and they can embrace its offerings. Put yourself out all over social media. Promote who you are, what you do, why you do it, the work you do, what you love doing it, the impact for other people, the opportunity to come do it with you as an employee or a partner. Promote it, promote it, promote it. If you're hesitant then I would urge you to ignore that little voice in your head that's

## [12:10 - 13:59]

Now, I want to raise an important point that you need to be aware of. You have to control the conversation by maintaining control of the things that you're promoting. You have to follow through with your promises. This is the number one reason marketing doesn't work for businesses is they promote something they don't do. Oh, we're fast. We're efficient. We're effective. We treat everybody like family. You can trust us. And they ignore the client the first time the phone rings or they don't follow up on a phone call. And they break every promise in the first engagement with the client. Don't do that. So whatever you're promoting without fail, make sure you're controlling the deliverable on the promote. Controlling the conversation takes an incredible amount of discipline as well as commitment to developing your organization's people and upholding and refining its processes and deliverables to people you serve. In the next few lessons, we'll dig into the three types of specific promotion you need to do now that you've taken over your business. First, you need to promote transparency. You're going to need to have full transparency across all spectrums of your business to be successful. You also want to promote the impact of your ownership to your team. What's in it for them to come on this journey with you? What are you going to give to them for helping you? And you want to promote the impact of your ownership to clients and to vendors. You want to reach out to people, have that big vision, be able to promote where you're going, why you're doing it, why you bought the business, what you're excited about and why they fit into that story. So we're going to be talking about those three types of promote in the next video. So we'll see you there.
